name: Mort's Quiche
recipeIngredient: Worcester sauce, soy sauce, teriyaki sauce (from a bottle)
Cinnamon (about 1/2 teaspoon for mushrooms, 1/4 teaspoon for onions)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clarify the butter by melting it and straining off the solids.
2. For the filling, cook mushrooms and onions separately in large and small frying pans with clarified butter, salt, pepper, and a mix of Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, teriyaki sauce, and cinnamon.
3. Cook mushrooms until very brown and caramelized, then combine with onions, which should be cooked until almost burnt.
4. Combine the cooked mushrooms and onions thoroughly.
5. For the pastry, mix flour, egg yolks, butter (cut into pieces), and brown sugar in a bowl. Knead to form a ball, adding more flour if necessary.
6. Roll out the dough on a floured surface until it is large enough for the quiche pan.
7. Trim the dough to fit inside the 10-11 inch quiche pan.
8. Place the dough into the prepared pan and trim any excess around the edges.
9. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
10. For the custard, mix sour cream or whipping cream with egg yolks, grated jack cheese, nutmeg, and a pinch of salt.
11. Assemble the quiche by placing cheddar cheese in the bottom shell, adding the mushroom/onion mixture, pouring the custard over everything, and finishing with more shredded cheddar and green onion chives.
12. Bake for 40-50 minutes until set and golden brown. Rotate halfway through cooking.
This recipe is attributed to Mortzco.
